english: Roti John with Mutton and Cheese

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for Roti John; mapped to the nearest parent class 'sandwich food product' as Roti John is an open-faced baguette sandwich. Exact Roti John entry absent from FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not contain a specific Roti John entry; mapped to the 'Sandwich' category (A0FJP) as the closest defensible structural match. Roti John is an open-faced baguette with egg-coated mutton and melted cheese.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept found for Roti John or sufficiently close Malay sandwich analogue as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Roti John Mutton with Cheese is approximately 180 g, equivalent to one half-baguette portion as commonly sold at Singaporean hawker stalls and food courts.

nutrient_source.title: Roti John (Mutton with Cheese) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 for all per_100g fields. Values are accepted as sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Roti John Mutton with Cheese. Saturated fat is not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central lamb mince and processed cheese composition data and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — white bread / baguette-based mixed dish analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Roti John Mutton with Cheese. GI estimated at 70 based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle being a white baguette (GI ~70 per University of Sydney GI Database for white French baguette). The protein and fat from mutton and cheese will moderately attenuate the postprandial glucose response in practice, but the structural GI anchor remains the white bread base. Glycemic load calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 28.13) / 100 ≈ 19.7 for a 180 g serving (28.13 g available carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (314 mg/100 g) compounded by cheese addition; low potassium (86 mg/100 g) provides insufficient offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Roti John Mutton with Cheese to occasional consumption, request reduced cheese quantity, avoid high-sodium condiment sauces, and note that a standard 180 g serving delivers approximately 565 mg sodium before condiments — nearly 25% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white baguette base (GI ~70) with moderate carbohydrate load (15.6 g/100 g); low dietary fibre (0.7 g/100 g) limits glucose modulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to half a standard serving, avoid sweet chilli or ketchup condiments that add free sugars, and pair with a fibre-rich side salad to blunt the postprandial glucose rise from the high-GI baguette base.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from mutton and cheese (~2.3 g/100 g), elevated sodium (314 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (34 mg/100 g) collectively raise cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should consume Roti John Mutton with Cheese sparingly, choose natural cheese over processed cheese to reduce sodium and saturated fat, and avoid high-fat condiment sauces to keep total saturated fat and sodium within daily cardiovascular targets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Mutton (red meat) is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), amplifying pro-inflammatory cytokine pathways; cheese adds saturated fat with no meaningful anti-inflammatory offset
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit Roti John Mutton with Cheese; substituting mutton with chicken or fish and reducing cheese quantity will significantly lower the pro-inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roti John Mutton with Cheese is a popular Singaporean Malay hawker dish consisting of a halved French baguette pan-fried with a seasoned minced mutton and egg mixture pressed onto the cut surface, then topped with melted processed or natural cheese. The bread becomes crispy on the outside while the egg-mutton coating remains moist and savoury. It is typically served with chilli sauce and/or mayonnaise. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 141 kcal, 6.8 g protein, 5.48 g fat, and 15.63 g carbohydrate, with 314 mg sodium — a level that warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension. The white baguette base carries a high glycemic index (~70), making portion control important for people with Type 2 Diabetes. The mutton component contributes arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acids), which may exacerbate inflammation in individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. Roti John is a culturally significant street food originating in Singapore and Malaysia, commonly sold at hawker centres, food courts, and pasar malam (night markets), and is considered a satisfying lunch or dinner snack.
common_user_queries: Roti John vs Roti John egg

confidence_reason: All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95. The HPB is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. Saturated fat is the only field estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and is clearly documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database white baguette entry, which is the appropriate approved GI source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient vitamins, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific Roti John Mutton with Cheese preparation are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry, and no sufficiently close analogue entry exists in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for this composite dish as of 2026. Saturated_fat_g (2.3 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for ground lamb (NDB 17066) and processed American cheese (NDB 01042) weighted by approximate ingredient proportions; this is an approximation and should be replaced with a direct HPB or laboratory value when available. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white French baguette from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Roti John Mutton with Cheese exists in the published literature as of 2026.
